title: Any way to get the exact complexity cost added by certain fields in the Monday GraphQL API?
excerpt: I am looking for something like Shopify’s 'X-GraphQL-Cost-Include-Fields': true header. Basically sending this in the request in Shopify will have the response from the server include the exact cost of every field in the…

posts.author: EricM (Eric)
posts.cookedHtml: I am looking for something like Shopify’s 'X-GraphQL-Cost-Include-Fields': true header. Basically sending this in the request in Shopify will have the response from the server include the exact cost of every field in the query (Shopify API rate limits). Is there anything similar for Monday? I am trying to calculate query costs client-side before sending the request to more precisely handle rate limits.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here you go:

monday API docs

Rate Limits

Learn more about how the monday.com GraphQL API implements rate limiting.

posts.author: EricM (Eric)
posts.cookedHtml:
I believe I understand how to calculate the cost in broad terms. Objects seem to have a fixed cost of 10, while fields have a cost of 1 (times any multiplier on the object).

I was looking for something more exact/specific. For example, Boards has an items_count field, which apparently “comes with a high complexity cost”. The cost for this specific field seems to be ‘5000’ (from testing in the playground). Is there a list of these “edge cases”?

image820×277 10.2 KB

monday API docs

Boards

monday.com boards are where users input all of their data, making them one of the core platform components. Items (rows), groups (groups of rows), and columns comprise the board's structure, and the board's data is stored in items and their...

New boards query field: items_count Announcements
Hello all! We have added a new field, items_count, to the boards query. This new field will return the number of items on boards across an account or across certain boards if specified in the query. The items_count field can be called like this: query { boards { items_count } } With this new field, you can enjoy more ease in working with board data, as you will no longer have to do a manual count of items in your account. Be sure to use the items_count query with caution,…

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @EricM,

We do not have a document with a list of complexity points costs per case right now.

I will share this with our team so that they can take it into account and hopefully add it in the future

Cheers,
Matias
